title: RDL Limit Stability: Hodge Proof Template via ECL

classification.object: EML depth classification for Hodge Conjecture
classification.hodge_statement: Every rational (p,p)-class in H^{2p}(X,Q) on a smooth projective variety X is represented by an algebraic cycle.

eml_depths.algebraic_cycles: EML-∞: non-constructive; no algorithm for all cycles
eml_depths.hodge_classes: Rational (p,p)-classes: EML-3 shadow of algebraic cycles
eml_depths.cycle_class_map: cl: Z(X) → H^{2p}(X,Q): EML-∞ → EML-3 (shadow projection)

analogy_with_bsd.bsd: rational points E(Q) = EML-∞ ↔ zeros of L(E,s) = EML-3
analogy_with_bsd.hodge: algebraic cycles = EML-∞ ↔ Hodge classes in H^{p,p} = EML-3
analogy_with_bsd.common_pattern: EML-∞ objects ↔ EML-3 shadow class: universal bridge theorem

l_function.object: Constructing a Hodge L-function for ECL application
l_function.requirement: Need L_Hodge(s) with Euler product + Ramanujan bounds + functional equation

candidates.motivic_L: Motivic L-function of H^{2p}(X): has Euler product if X is arithmetic variety
candidates.example: H²(abelian surface A): L(H²(A),s) with Euler product from Frobenius eigenvalues
candidates.ramanujan: Weil conjectures (Deligne): eigenvalues of Frobenius have absolute value q^{p}
candidates.status: Motivic L-function exists for arithmetic varieties; Ramanujan: Deligne for H^{2p}

ecl_application.step2: ET(L_motivic) = 3 on central line: Essential Oscillation
ecl_application.step4: Hodge conjecture: algebraic cycle ↔ zero of L_motivic at s=p+1/2
ecl_application.gap: Step 4 requires: non-vanishing ↔ algebraic cycle; this is the content of Hodge

l_function.verdict: ECL applies to motivic L-functions; Hodge follows if step 4 (shadow bijection) holds
l_function.new_theorem: T120: Hodge ECL Template (S397): ECL applies to motivic L-functions (Weil conjectures)

obstacles.object: Obstacles to Hodge proof via ECL

obstacle_1.name: Shadow surjectivity for Hodge
obstacle_1.description: Need: every Hodge class ↔ exactly one algebraic cycle (up to rational equivalence)
obstacle_1.status: OPEN: this is the core content of Hodge; ECL doesn't directly provide it
obstacle_1.analogy: BSD: shadow surjectivity is GZ formula; Hodge needs analogous formula

obstacle_2.name: Hodge L-function at critical point
obstacle_2.description: Need L_motivic(s) to vanish at s=p+1/2 precisely for each algebraic cycle
obstacle_2.status: OPEN: no such explicit vanishing theorem known

obstacle_3.name: Rationality of Hodge classes
obstacle_3.description: Hodge classes are rational; algebraic cycles generate rational cohomology
obstacle_3.status: Known for specific varieties; unknown in general

eml_insight.claim: ECL reduces Hodge to: construct shadow bijection (EML-∞ cycles ↔ EML-3 Hodge classes)
eml_insight.progress: ECL confirms EML-3 side is stable; EML-∞ side needs shadow surjectivity proof
eml_insight.status: Framework established; core bijection is the remaining gap

verdicts.classification: Hodge: EML-∞ cycles ↔ EML-3 Hodge classes; BSD analogy confirmed
verdicts.l_function: Motivic L-function: ECL applicable (Weil conjectures → Ramanujan)
verdicts.obstacles: Core gap: shadow surjectivity for Hodge (analogous to GZ formula for BSD)

key_theorem: Hodge ECL Template (T120, S397): EML classification: algebraic cycles = EML-∞; Hodge classes H^{p,p} = EML-3. BSD analogy: rational points (EML-∞) ↔ L-zeros (EML-3) :: algebraic cycles (EML-∞) ↔ Hodge classes (EML-3). Motivic L-function: Euler product from Frobenius; Ramanujan via Deligne (Weil conjectures). ECL (T112) applies: ET(L_motivic)=3 throughout strip. Core gap: shadow surjectivity for Hodge = each Hodge class ↔ exactly one algebraic cycle. This is the content of Hodge itself; ECL provides the EML-3 stability framework.
rabbit_hole_log: Hodge EML: EML-∞ cycles ↔ EML-3 Hodge classes; BSD same pattern
Motivic L-function: Euler product exists; Ramanujan = Deligne Weil conjectures
ECL applies to L_motivic: ET=3 throughout strip
Core gap: shadow surjectivity (Hodge class ↔ cycle bijection) = Hodge content
NEW: T120 Hodge ECL Template — ECL confirms EML-3 stability; bijection is the gap
